authorities.name: U.S. Geological Survey (Geographic Names Information System)

sources.title: State of Nevada Board of Wildlife Commissioners — Fishing Seasons CR 25-16 (Jan 1, 2026 – Dec 31, 2027), Washoe County Closed Waters
sources.quotedText: CLOSED WATERS: Third Creek and its tributaries from Lake Tahoe to the Mt. Rose Highway No. 431. Incline Creek and its tributaries from Lake Tahoe to Ski Way and the Mt. Rose Highway No. 431. Wood Creek from Lake Tahoe to the Mt. Rose Highway No. 431. Lake Tahoe within a 200-yard radius of the mouths of Third, Incline and Wood creeks. A 500-yard radius from Sand Harbor boat ramp.

sources.title: State of Nevada Board of Wildlife Commissioners — Fishing Seasons CR 25-16 (Jan 1, 2026 – Dec 31, 2027), Washoe County (Lake Tahoe and tributaries)
sources.quotedText: Lake Tahoe and tributaries. Open year around except for closed areas. Daily: 5 game fish. Possession: 10 game fish. 1 hour before sunrise to 2 hours after sunset.

sources.title: Apply & Buy — Nevada Fishing Licenses — NDOW
sources.quotedText: Anyone 12 years of age or older is required to have a fishing license in their possession while in the field.

sources.quotedText: Third Creek near Crystal Bay, Nev.

sources.quotedText: The list, below, groups rivers and creeks that flow into the lake by their locations on the north, east, south and west shores, in a clockwise order.

sources.title: USGS GNIS (The National Map Gazetteer) — Streams layer record for gaz_id 861713, "Third Creek", Stream, Washoe County, NV
sources.quotedText: {"gaz_id":861713,"gaz_name":"Third Creek","gaz_featureclass":"Stream","fcode":46000,"state_alpha":"NV","county_name":"Washoe"} — geometry multipoint; mouth point (Web Mercator -13352578.8917, 4755895.916) converts to lon -119.94826, lat 39.23852, source point (Web Mercator -13350971.2277, 4768000.3424) converts to lon -119.93382, lat 39.32269. The file previously recorded -119.94545, 39.26346 (an unsourced estimate, ~2.8 km from the GNIS mouth point and ~6.6 km from the GNIS source point); corrected to the GNIS mouth coordinate.

parameters.note: Third Creek's lower/main reach — from its mouth at Lake Tahoe up to the Mt. Rose Highway No. 431, which is the entire reach flowing through Incline Village, including this dataset's representative point — is CLOSED WATERS year-round under NDOW's Washoe County closed-waters listing in CR 25-16 (protecting spawning fish; Third Creek is a Lake Tahoe kokanee/trout spawning tributary). Lake Tahoe itself within a 200-yard radius of the Third Creek mouth is also closed. Only the headwater segment above the Mt. Rose Highway 431 crossing is not covered by this closure; there the general 'Lake Tahoe and tributaries' open-year-round regime applies (see the other rules on this record).

regulations.citation: NBWC CR 25-16 — Washoe County, Closed Waters
regulations.humanSummary: Third Creek is closed to all fishing year-round from Lake Tahoe upstream to the Mt. Rose Highway No. 431 (the entire reach through Incline Village, including this point). Lake Tahoe within 200 yards of the creek mouth is also closed.
regulations.verbatimText: Third Creek and its tributaries from Lake Tahoe to the Mt. Rose Highway No. 431.

start.verbatim: Open year around except for closed areas

end.verbatim: Open year around except for closed areas

parameters.note: Applies ONLY to Third Creek above the Mt. Rose Highway 431 closure boundary. As a Lake Tahoe tributary in Washoe County, that non-closed headwater segment falls under NDOW's 'Lake Tahoe and tributaries' entry (CR 25-16), which is open year around 'except for closed areas.' The closed area for Third Creek is the entire reach from Lake Tahoe to Mt. Rose Highway 431 (see the closure rule) — so in practice the fishable, named lower creek is closed year-round and this open season covers only the small headwater portion above the highway.

regulations.citation: NBWC CR 25-16 — Washoe County (Lake Tahoe and tributaries)
regulations.humanSummary: Above the Mt. Rose Highway 431 closure, Third Creek (as a Lake Tahoe tributary) is open year-round; the reach below the highway is closed.
regulations.verbatimText: Lake Tahoe and tributaries. Open year around except for closed areas.

parameters.counting_rule: No species-specific sub-limit is stated for the 'Lake Tahoe and tributaries' entry; the limit is a combined game-fish total. Applies only to the non-closed headwater segment above Mt. Rose Highway 431 — the reach below the highway is closed to all take.

regulations.citation: NBWC CR 25-16 — Washoe County (Lake Tahoe and tributaries)
regulations.humanSummary: Where open (above Mt. Rose Highway 431): 5 game fish daily, 10 in possession.
regulations.verbatimText: Daily: 5 game fish. Possession: 10 game fish.

parameters.note: Hours for the 'Lake Tahoe and tributaries' entry (CR 25-16, Washoe Co.). Relevant only to the non-closed headwater segment above Mt. Rose Highway 431.

regulations.citation: NBWC CR 25-16 — Washoe County (Lake Tahoe and tributaries)
regulations.humanSummary: Where open: fishing hours are 1 hour before sunrise to 2 hours after sunset.
regulations.verbatimText: 1 hour before sunrise to 2 hours after sunset.

parameters.note: Standard Nevada resident/nonresident fishing license required for anglers 12 and older. Third Creek is a Nevada tributary stream (not the Lake Tahoe reciprocal boundary water itself), so the California-license reciprocity that applies on Lake Tahoe proper does not extend to fishing the creek. Note the fishable lower reach is closed to all fishing regardless (see the closure rule).

regulations.humanSummary: A Nevada fishing license is required for anglers age 12 and older; no license required under age 12.
regulations.verbatimText: Anyone 12 years of age or older is required to have a fishing license in their possession while in the field.
